zotero.abstractNote: The Nesterov's Accelerated Gradient (NAG) method is a cornerstone of optimization in machine learning. Recently, the Nesterov's Gradient Normalization (NGN) method has been proposed as a robust alternative, which is invariant to the scale of the gradient. However, NGN requires a careful choice of the step-size, which is often difficult to tune. In this paper, we propose a momentum-based adaptation of the NGN step-size, which we call Momentum-NGN (M-NGN). We show that M-NGN is robust to the choice of the step-size and achieves state-of-the-art performance on a variety of optimization problems, including deep learning and convex optimization. We also provide a theoretical analysis of the convergence of M-NGN and show that it achieves the optimal convergence rate for smooth convex functions.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
